steps.title: Turn the information into two points
steps.content: The function has the form $j(x)=mx+170$, so its y-intercept is $170$. That gives the point $(0,170)$. The statement $j(14)=23$ gives the point $(14,23)$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content:
Use the slope formula with $(0,170)$ and $(14,23)$:
$$m=\frac{23-170}{14-0}=\frac{-147}{14}=-10.5.$$ Since the intercept is already $170$, $$j(x)=-10.5x+170.$$
